title: Meat Selection and Preparation
content:
MEAT SELECTION

Tender cuts: From loin and prime rib; mild flavor; short cooking with dry heat (broiling, roasting). Reddish color indicates meat from young animal.

Tougher cuts: More connective tissue; longer cooking with moist heat to tenderize and develop flavor. Cut across grain for tenderness.

Quality Indicators:
- Beef: Bright cherry red, pliable yet firm; fat white, firm, brittle
- Veal: Pale grayish pink, finer grain than beef; fat clear, hard, white to slightly pink
- Pork: Nearly white (young) to pink (older); fine-grained, juicy, velvety; fat white, firm, smooth
- Lamb: Pinkish red; firm, juicy, velvety; fat white, clear, brittle, flaky

PREPARATION

Wipe with clean, damp cloth; do not wash. Trim excess fat (prevents flare-ups in broiling). Where possible, leave bone for flavor; remove for rolled roasts or stuffed cuts.

French chops: Scrape meat from end of bone; place paper frill over bone end after broiling.
